title: Async Context, Abort Signals, Deadlines, and Cleanup · Inference Brief: Async Local Storage Scope
summary: Request context is stored globally, promises continue after disconnect, timeouts race without cancellation, dependency budgets consume cleanup time, and rejected work becomes unhandled. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts async local storage scope increment in request context, budget, cancellation, and cleanup ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use AsyncLocalStorage run, enterWith limits, snapshot, name, and bounded request metadata without treating context as authorization.
Serve downloads with authorized resource lookup, content disposition, safe filenames, media type, validators, ranges, HEAD parity, byte accounting, and abort cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Async Local Storage Scope · Theory Async Local Storage Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3160 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reserve cleanup time and combine client abort with one dependency budget; retain compatibility with one older generated client; then replace an average latency claim with p95 and event-loop saturation evidence. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
